content.text:
<user_query>
There is currently a blocking issue when using the certification request wizard. After I select my intent in the first step, I expect to go to the second step.

For intents that require product selection, select the product. For requests that require only additional context, provide that context. Instead, we are always redirected to step 3.

This is the case because when adding an already existing request, it is intended—or the best approach—to go directly to step 3 to review the drafts. Of course, this should only happen if the user has already completed step 2.

Therefore, the data should be used to determine which step to go to based on the state of the data. Alternatively, we should track which steps have already been visited in the data context to decide whether to redirect to step 3 when first editing a new or existing certification request.

This currently only occurs when using the certification request wizard inside the authenticated application.@Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/pages/RequestCreationPage.tsx @Procertus-ui-experiment/apps/frontend-pt1-extranet-onboarding/src/pages/RequestEditPage.tsx 
</user_query>

Tracing the certification request wizard flow to find why we always jump to step 3 after step 1.

[REDACTED]

RequestEditPage hardcodes `initialStep="drafts"`. Exploring the wizard in `@procertus-ui/ui-certification` to determine how steps map to data.

[REDACTED]

Inspecting draft shape after intent vs after details and how inquiries map from the authenticated store.

[REDACTED]

Checking how the localStorage backend merges persisted sessions with `initialSession` — persisted `activeStep` may override the computed initial step.

[REDACTED]

Identifying issues:
1. `RequestEditPage` always passes `initialStep="drafts"`.
2. `normalizeInitialStep` defaults to drafts whenever any drafts exist.
3. `createDraftFromIntent` sets `activeStep: Math.max(current.activeStep, 1)` — correct for details.
4. Persisted sessions may retain a stale `activeStep`; adding a reconciliation effect.

Implementing `certificationInquiriesNeedDetailsStep` and updating `normalizeInitialStep` plus a layout effect to reconcile persisted state.

[REDACTED]

Implementing the helper and fixing `normalizeInitialStep` plus a reconciliation effect for persisted sessions.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Seeding session `intent` from drafts so the details step works when `canOpenDetails` requires `intent`. Merging reconciliation into one effect and fixing dependency stability.

[REDACTED]

Removing `updateSession` from effect dependencies — it's a new function reference each render and would retrigger effects.

[REDACTED]
